name: @tinyhttp/app (Minimal Express-like Framework)

performance.notes: Faster than Express; uses native Node.js without legacy baggage

agent_readiness.notes: Express-like Node.js framework without legacy baggage. import { App } from '@tinyhttp/app'. const app = new App(). app.get('/', (req, res) => res.send('Hello')). app.post('/user', async (req, res) => { res.json(await db.save(req.body)) }). app.use(logger()). app.listen(3000). Middleware compatible with Express middleware. Built-in async error handling. req.params, req.query, req.body (via @tinyhttp/json). TypeScript-first. No legacy connect middleware compat layer — pure Node.js http module. Router: new Router(). Sub-apps: app.use('/api', apiApp). 0 dependencies for core.

gotchas: Smaller community than Express, Fastify, or Hono
Not all Express middleware is compatible (connect-based middleware won't work)
Less battle-tested in production at scale
Hono covers similar space with better edge/multi-runtime support
best_when: Lightweight Node.js APIs wanting Express ergonomics without the baggage
TypeScript-first projects that find Express types cumbersome
avoid_when: Large teams needing deep ecosystem (use Express or Fastify)
When legacy Express middleware compatibility is required
summary: tinyhttp is a zero-dependency Express-like TypeScript Node.js framework. new App(); app.get('/', (req, res) => res.send('Hello')); app.listen(3000). Express-compatible ergonomics, async error handling, TypeScript-first. MIT. Faster than Express. Smaller community than Fastify or Hono. Not all Express (connect) middleware compatible. Best for lightweight TypeScript APIs wanting Express feel. Prefer Hono for edge/multi-runtime.
